questions.question: Define a Binary Search Tree (BST) and explain its properties.
keyPoints: Properties of a BST: unique values, left subtree values are less than the node's value, right subtree values are greater than the node's value

questions.question: Explain the search operation in a Binary Search Tree (BST) and provide an example.
keyPoints: Explanation of the search operation in a BST
Example of searching for a value in a BST
Time complexity of the search operation in a BST

questions.question: Compare and contrast Binary Search Trees (BSTs) with Hash Tables.
keyPoints: Comparison of BSTs and Hash Tables: search, insert, delete operations
Advantages and disadvantages of using BSTs and Hash Tables
Time and space complexity of BSTs and Hash Tables

questions.question: List the advantages and disadvantages of using Binary Search Trees (BSTs).
keyPoints: Advantages of BSTs: efficient search, insert, and delete operations
Disadvantages of BSTs: can become unbalanced, requires additional memory

questions.question: Explain how to implement a Binary Search Tree (BST) in code and provide an example.
keyPoints: Explanation of implementing a BST in code
Example of implementing a BST in code
Time and space complexity of the implemented BST
